column1: # Targeted web research for high-priority exchanges with thin coverage
# Method: WebSearch + WebFetch across OFAC SDN
# - FTX: Existing 47 addresses cover known public wallets; FTX stored keys in AWS hot wallets
#   and made little use of cold storage per bankruptcy debtors. No new BTC addresses found.
# - Binance US: Operates under BAM Trading; SEC filings show custody key disputes but no
#   public BTC address disclosures beyond what Binance.com published. No new addresses found.
# - Celsius: Custody via Fireblocks/BitGo; Ionic Digital mining successor identified by Arkham
#   but BTC addresses not published in accessible sources. No new addresses found.
# - Voyager: Commingled wallet structure with Ledger Vault custody; no public BTC addresses
#   beyond existing 2 in dataset. No new addresses found.
# - Upbit: "Mr. 100" cold wallet (1Ay8vMC7R1UbyCCZRVULMV7iQpHSAbguJP) already in dataset.
#   Additional wallets tracked by Arkham/CoinCarp but addresses not accessible. No new found.

column2: 993 BTC to 70 addresses sent to YoBit; these are hacker
not Bithumb's own wallets. No new Bithumb addresses found.
MEXC: Proof-of-reserves pages require JS rendering; addresses not extractable.

column1: # - ChangeNOW: Only ETH/EVM hot wallets publicly labeled; no BTC addresses found.
# - BitMart: Dec 2021 hack was ETH/BSC only
# - Galaxy Digital: Institutional custody via BitGo; no public BTC wallet disclosures.
# - Cash App: Individual user addresses rotate per transaction; no corporate BTC addresses public.
